mnemonics: Twisted Pairs Twist Signals, Coaxial Cables Coat Signals, Fiber Optics Flash Signals
keyPoints: Twisted Pair Cable uses copper wires twisted together to reduce electromagnetic interference.
Coaxial Cable has a central conductor surrounded by an insulating layer, a braided metal shield, and an outer cover.
Fiber Optic Cable transmits data as pulses of light through glass or plastic fibers, offering high bandwidth and immunity to EMI.
commonMistakes: Confusing the use cases: assuming twisted pair is always sufficient for long distances (fiber is better) or that coaxial is obsolete (it's still used for cable internet).
Thinking fiber optic cable is immune to all damage; it is fragile and can be broken by excessive bending.
